name: app.R
content:
## Shiny app for changepoint detection and visualization ---------------------- ## Load Packages --------------------------------------------------------------- library(shiny) library(bslib) library(ggplot2) library(readxl) library(changepoint) library(dplyr) library(rmarkdown) library(writexl) ## helper functions ----------------------------------------------------- #' Estimate change points in dataset #' #' @param data A data frame with columns: date, measurement, variable #' @param pen.val Penalty value for PELT algorithm #' @return A data frame with changepoint dates and mean values for each segment estChange <- function(data, pen.val) { ### Estimate changepoint in dataset fit_changepoint = cpt.mean(data$measurement / sd(data$measurement), method = "PELT", penalty = "Manual", pen.value = paste(pen.val, "* log(n)")) # Extract estimated changepoints and mean values for each segment changepoints <- cpts(fit_changepoint) mean_values <- param.est(fit_changepoint)$mean * sd(data$measurement) # corresponding dates of changepoints changepoint_dates <- as.Date(sort(data$date[changepoints])) # changepoint location is the last observation in segment # dataframe for visualization purposes with changepoint-means in the segments return(data.frame(variable = unique(data$variable), segment = seq(1:(length(changepoint_dates) + 1)), date_start = c(min(data$date), changepoint_dates+1), date_end = c(changepoint_dates, max(data$date)), #mean_value = round(mean_values, 2))) mean_value = mean_values)) } ## Define UI ------------------------------------------------------------------- ui <- page_sidebar( title = "Changepoint Detection and Visualization", sidebar = sidebar( width = 450, h5("1. Upload Data", style = "margin-top: -20px; margin-bottom: -5px;"), div(p("The dataset must be provided as an Excel file (.xlsx) containing the following 4 columns: ", tags$ul( tags$li(tags$b("variable"), " (measured variable, e.g. laboratory endpoint)"), tags$li(tags$b("date"), " (date of the measurement)"), tags$li(tags$b("measurement"), " (numeric measurement value)"), tags$li(tags$b("group"), " (optional grouping variable used to color observations, e.g. company)") )), style = "font-size: 0.9em; color: #666; margin-bottom: 10px;"), fileInput( "file", NULL, accept = c(".xlsx", ".xls"), buttonLabel = "Browse...", placeholder = "No file selected" ), div( style = "display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-top: 10px;", actionButton( "use_example", "Use example data", class = "btn btn-light btn-sm", style = "flex-grow: 1; color: #555;" ), downloadButton( "download_example", label = NULL, icon = icon("download"), class = "btn btn-sm", style = " padding: 6px 10px; background: transparent; border: none; color: #6c757d; box-shadow: none; " ), actionLink( "example_info", label = NULL, icon = icon("info-circle"), title = "About this dataset", style = "color: #007bff;" ) ), hr(style = "margin-top: -10px; margin-bottom: -5px;"), h5("2. Detect Changepoints", style = "margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: -20px;"), p( "Choose a penalty value to control the sensitivity of the changepoint detection: lower values detect minor shifts, higher values detect only major changes.", style = "font-size: 0.9em; color: #666; margin-bottom: 10px;" ), selectInput( "pen_value", label = tags$span( "Penalty Value:", actionLink( "pen_info", label = NULL, icon = icon("question-circle"), style = "margin-left: 6px; color: #007bff;" ) ), choices = c( "2 - BIC" = 2, "5 - high sensitivity (more changepoints)" = 5, 10, 15, "25 - medium sensitivity (default)" = 25, "40 - low sensitivity (fewer changepoints)" = 40), selected = 25 ), actionButton( "calc_changepoints", "Calculate changepoints", class = "btn-success w-100", icon = icon("chart-area") ), tags$style(HTML(" #var_order + .selectize-control .selectize-input { background-color: #f5f6f8 !important; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0 !important; box-shadow: none !important; border-radius: 6px !important; font-size: 12px !important; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } #var_order + .selectize-control .selectize-input.focus { border-color: #b0b0b0 !important; box-shadow: none !important; } #var_order + .selectize-control .selectize-dropdown { border: 1px solid #e0e0e0 !important; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) !important; font-size: 12px !important; } #var_order + .selectize-control .option:hover { background-color: #f0f2f5 !important; } #download_report:hover { background-color: #e9ecef !important; border-color: #ced4da !important; color: #212529 !important; } ")), hr(style = "margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: -5px;"), h5("3. Download Report", style = "margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: -20px;"), p( "Run steps 1 and 2 before downloading the report.", style = "font-size: 0.9em; color: #666; margin-bottom: 10px;" ), downloadButton( "download_report", "Generate report", class = "btn w-100", icon = icon("file-download"), style = " background-color: #f8f9fa; border: 1px solid #dee2e6; color: #495057; " ) ), card( height = "1000px", card_header( div( style = "display: flex; align-items: center; width: 100%;", span("Data visualization"), actionLink( "plot_info", label = NULL, icon = icon("info-circle"), title = "Visualization options", style = "margin-left: 8px; color: white;" ), div( style = "margin-left: auto; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;", uiOutput("var_order_ui"), uiOutput("reset_zoom_ui") ) ), class = "bg-primary" ), card_body( style = "overflow-y: auto;", # Adds scrollbar if needed plotOutput( "scatterplot", height = "700px", brush = brushOpts(id = "plot_brush", resetOnNew = FALSE) ) ) ), card( height = "600px", card_header( "Changepoints by variable", class = "bg-success" ), card_body( tableOutput("changepoints_table") ) ) ) ## Define server --------------------------------------------------------------- server <- function(input, output, session) { # zoom in plot zoom_state <- reactiveValues( x = NULL, y = NULL ) data <- reactiveVal(NULL) dataset <- reactiveVal(NULL) observeEvent(input$file, { req(input$file) df <- read_excel(input$file$datapath) data(df) changepoints_calculated(FALSE) }) observeEvent(input$use_example, { load("data/example_dataset_changepoint.RData") data(data_for_shiny_5var) changepoints_calculated(FALSE) }) ### # Store the penalty value used for the last changepoint calculation last_pen_value <- reactiveVal(NULL) # Track whether changepoints have been calculated for current file changepoints_calculated <- reactiveVal(FALSE) # Reset changepoints_calculated when new file is uploaded # observeEvent(input$file, { # changepoints_calculated(FALSE) # }) # Update last penalty value when calc_changepoints is clicked observeEvent(input$calc_changepoints, { last_pen_value(as.numeric(input$pen_value)) changepoints_calculated(TRUE) }) # Reactive for changepoints changepoints_data <- reactive({ req(changepoints_calculated()) req(last_pen_value()) # Require that a penalty value has been set data <- isolate(data()) data <- data() pen_value <- last_pen_value() # Calculate changepoints for each variable variables <- unique(data$variable) cp_results <- lapply(variables, function(var) { var_data <- data %>% filter(variable == var) %>% arrange(date) estChange(var_data, pen.val = pen_value) }) # Combine results and remove NULL entries cp_combined <- bind_rows(cp_results[!sapply(cp_results, is.null)]) cp_combined }) # Order of the variables in the plot cp_rank <- reactive({ req(changepoints_calculated()) cp_data <- changepoints_data() cp_data %>% group_by(variable) %>% slice_max(segment, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>% arrange(desc(date_start)) %>% pull(variable) }) latest_cp_text_reactive <- reactive({ req(changepoints_calculated()) cp_data <- changepoints_data() if (!is.null(cp_data) && nrow(cp_data) > 0) { latest_cp <- cp_data %>% group_by(variable) %>% slice_max(segment, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>% arrange(desc(date_start)) %>% slice(1) sprintf( "Most recent estimated changepoint: %s at %s", latest_cp$variable, format(as.Date(latest_cp$date_start), "%Y-%m-%d") ) } else { "No changepoints detected." } }) # Reactive for creating the plot (reusable for display and report) plot_output <- reactive({ #req(input$file) # Only require file upload req(data()) data <- data() # Trigger redraw when file changes (this ensures plot redraws completely) input$file # Create base plot #p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = date, y = measurement, color = group)) + use_group <- !all(is.na(data$group)) # --- VARIABLE ORDER LOGIC --- if (!changepoints_calculated() || is.null(input$var_order) || input$var_order == "alpha") { var_levels <- sort(unique(data$variable)) } else { var_levels <- cp_rank() } data$variable <- factor(data$variable, levels = var_levels) p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = date, y = measurement, color = if(use_group) group else NULL)) + geom_point(size = 1.3, alpha = 0.7) + facet_grid(rows = vars(variable), scales = "free_y") + labs( title = "Measurements over time, separated by variable and colored by group", x = "Date", y = "Measurement", color = "Group" ) + theme_minimal() + theme( strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0, size = 10), strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = "grey50"), # legend.position = "bottom", legend.position = if(length(unique(na.omit(data$group))) <= 10) "bottom" else "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14), axis.text = element_text(size = 10), axis.title = element_text(size = 12) ) + guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 6))) # Add changepoint lines if calc_changepoints was triggered if (changepoints_calculated()) { cp_data <- changepoints_data() cp_data$variable <- factor(cp_data$variable, levels = var_levels) if (!is.null(cp_data) && nrow(cp_data) > 0) { p <- p + geom_segment( data = cp_data, aes(x = date_start, xend = date_end, y = mean_value, yend = mean_value), color = "navy", linewidth = 1.3, alpha = 0.75, inherit.aes = FALSE ) } } p }) # Create plot when button is clicked output$scatterplot <- renderPlot({ p <- plot_output() # Zoom nur aus gespeichertem Zustand if (!is.null(zoom_state$x)) { p <- p + coord_cartesian( xlim = zoom_state$x ) } p }) # Display changepoints table output$changepoints_table <- renderTable({ # Require button click req(changepoints_calculated()) # Get changepoints cp_data <- changepoints_data() if (is.null(cp_data) || nrow(cp_data) == 0) { data.frame(Message = "No changepoints detected in any variable") } else { # Format the table with dates as character strings cp_summary <- cp_data %>% mutate( segment_start = as.character(as.Date(date_start)), segment_end = as.character(as.Date(date_end)), segment_mean = mean_value ) %>% dplyr::select(variable, segment, segment_start, segment_end, segment_mean) %>% rename( "Variable" = variable, "Segment" = segment, "Segment Start" = segment_start, "Segment End" = segment_end, "Segment Mean" = segment_mean ) cp_summary } }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, bordered = TRUE) # Download report output$download_report <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste("changepoint_report_", Sys.Date(), ".html", sep = "") }, content = function(file) { # Require that changepoints have been calculated req(input$calc_changepoints > 0) # Get the plot and table data plot_obj <- plot_output() cp_data <- changepoints_data() if (is.null(cp_data) || nrow(cp_data) == 0) { cp_table <- data.frame(Message = "No changepoints detected in any variable") } else { cp_table <- cp_data %>% mutate( segment_start = date_start, segment_end = date_end, segment_mean = mean_value ) %>% dplyr::select(variable, segment, segment_start, segment_end, segment_mean) %>% rename( "Variable" = variable, "Segment" = segment, "Segment Start" = segment_start, "Segment End" = segment_end, "Segment Mean" = segment_mean ) } # Create a temporary R Markdown file temp_report <- tempfile(fileext = ".Rmd") # Write the R Markdown content with params defined in YAML writeLines(c( "---", "title: 'Changepoint Detection Report'", paste0("date: '", format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"), "'"), "output: html_document", "params:", " plot_obj: NA", " cp_table: NA", " pen_value: NA", " file_name: NA", " latest_cp_text: NA", "---", "", "```{r setup, include=FALSE}", "knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)", "library(ggplot2)", "library(knitr)", "library(dplyr)", "```", "", "## Analysis Settings", "", paste0("**Data File:** `r params$file_name`"), "", paste0("**Penalty Value:** `r params$pen_value`"), "", "## Visualization", "", "```{r plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}", "params$plot_obj", "```", "", "## Changepoints Summary", "", "```{r table}", "kable(params$cp_table, caption = 'Detected changepoints by variable')", "", "## Summary", "", "```{r latest_cp}", "cat(params$latest_cp_text)", "```" ), temp_report) # Render the report with params rmarkdown::render( temp_report, output_file = file, params = list( plot_obj = plot_obj, cp_table = cp_table, pen_value = last_pen_value(), # Use the stored penalty value from last calculation file_name = if (!is.null(input$file)) input$file$name else "Example dataset", latest_cp_text = latest_cp_text_reactive() ) ) } ) observeEvent(input$pen_info, { showModal(modalDialog( title = "Penalty Information", HTML("

The penalty value controls how many changepoints are detected.

  • Lower values → higher sensitivity (more changepoints)
  • Higher values → lower sensitivity (fewer changepoints)

A value of 2 corresponds to the standard Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC), which assumes independent observations.

In case of dependency structures in the data, the BIC penalty may be overly sensitive, leading to the detection of too many changepoints.

In such cases, the penalty parameter can be increased relative to the BIC penalty.

"), easyClose = TRUE, footer = modalButton("Close") )) }) output$download_example <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { "example_dataset_changepoint.xlsx" }, content = function(file) { # Lade das Beispiel-Dataset load("data/example_dataset_changepoint.RData") # Schreibe als Excel-Datei writexl::write_xlsx(dplyr::select(data_for_shiny_5var, variable, date, measurement, group), path = file) } ) observeEvent(input$example_info, { showModal(modalDialog( title = "Example Dataset Information", HTML(" This dataset is provided for demonstration purposes to illustrate changepoint detection in time series data.

It contains data from five laboratory endpoints derived from control animals in the VICT3R database (https://www.vict3r.eu/). The dataset has the following characteristics:
  • Origin: multiple pharmaceutical companies (\"groups\", labelled A–J)
  • Study type: 28-day toxicity studies
  • Species: male Sprague-Dawley rats
The dataset can be downloaded using the button. "), easyClose = TRUE, footer = modalButton("Close") )) }) output$var_order_ui <- renderUI({ req(changepoints_calculated()) # nur anzeigen wenn TRUE selectInput( "var_order", label = NULL, choices = c( "Alphabetical" = "alpha", "Changepoint (latest)" = "cp" ), selected = "alpha", width = "170px" ) }) observeEvent(input$file, { req(input$file) df <- read_excel(input$file$datapath) data(df) changepoints_calculated(FALSE) updateSelectInput(session, "var_order", selected = "alpha") }) observeEvent(input$file, { req(input$file) df <- read_excel(input$file$datapath) data(df) changepoints_calculated(FALSE) updateSelectInput(session, "var_order", selected = "alpha") }) observeEvent(input$plot_brush, { zoom_state$x <- as.Date( c(input$plot_brush$xmin, input$plot_brush$xmax), origin = "1970-01-01" ) # Brush zurücksetzen session$resetBrush("plot_brush") }) observeEvent(input$reset_zoom, { zoom_state$x <- NULL }) output$reset_zoom_ui <- renderUI({ # Nur anzeigen wenn gezoomt wurde if (is.null(zoom_state$x)) { return(NULL) } actionButton( "reset_zoom", label = NULL, icon = icon("search-minus"), title = "Reset Zoom", class = "btn btn-outline-light", style = "padding: 6px 10px;" ) }) observeEvent(input$plot_info, { showModal(modalDialog( title = "Zoom & Display Options", HTML(" Zoom function
Select a region along the x-axis in the plot to zoom in.
Use the reset button (on the right) to return to the full view.

Variable ordering (when changepoints are shown)
You can change the order of variables in the plot:
  • Alphabetical order
  • Changepoint-based order (most recent changepoint at the top)
"), easyClose = TRUE )) }) } ## Run app --------------------------------------------------------------------- shinyApp(ui, server)
